summary: Patrick briefly shares that he nailed a job interview, noting that his current boss's relationship with the prospective employer and a former coworker's hiring authority helped. He uses this as a springboard to make a broader point about professional networking: the surprisingly high probability that people in related fields will cross paths again is reason enough to maintain impeccable professional conduct at all times.
key_points: Personal connections and prior working relationships significantly influence hiring outcomes
The importance of networking in hiring is common in both Japan and America, though Americans tend to downplay it
The high likelihood of re-encountering people in your field is a strong practical argument for being consistently polite and fair
money_quotes: The freakishly high chances that two people in a related field will again do business with each other are as good a reason as anything to be faultlessly polite and fair in all of your business dealings.
key_insight: The professional world is small enough that you should treat every business interaction as if you'll encounter that person again — because you probably will.
